fields.name: Street Captain
fields.description: Runs a mid-tier crew. Methodical and hard to rattle. He's been in more fights than you've eaten hot meals.

fields.title: Corner Tax
fields.description: An east-side crew has been running protection on shops in your territory. Time to remind them where the lines are.

fields.title: Crescent Street
fields.body: You find the captain finishing his rounds at the bodega on Crescent. Two runners flank him, but he sends them off when he clocks you coming. He's not worried. He should think harder about that.

fields.title: Squaring Off
fields.body: He moves first — a short jab that says he does this for a living. You settle in.

fields.title: Reminder
fields.body: He's better than you today. You end up against the bodega's shuttered front while he steps over you and tells his runners to move it. He doesn't even take your wallet. Worse — he doesn't need to.

fields.title: The Line
fields.body: He doesn't get up quickly. When he does, it's slow and deliberate. He says nothing. Neither do you. The bodega owner watches from behind the glass. Word will travel — the east side crew got reminded where the line is.

fields.title: The Talk
fields.body: You lay it out. His crew is running tax on the wrong side of Crescent, and that ends tonight. He listens. Whether he thinks you're the kind of person who can back that up is another question.

fields.title: No Blood
fields.body: He measures you for a long moment, then tells his runners to move it. They go. No blood, no debt — just a line redrawn without anyone having to fall. The bodega owner nods at you through the window.

fields.label: Step to him directly.
fields.arrival_flavor: You cross the street. He turns to face you without hurrying.

fields.label: Talk first.
fields.arrival_flavor: He watches you approach. Arms loose at his sides.

fields.label: Drag yourself home.
fields.arrival_flavor: You take the back streets. Nobody needs to see this.

fields.label: Head back to the corner.
fields.arrival_flavor: You walk back without looking behind you. You don't need to.

fields.label: Back to the square.
fields.arrival_flavor: You leave him with something to think about.
